{"path": "C:\Users\aralm\repo\custom-agents\agents\plan.md", "content": "# Plan: Add Device-Specific Playback to Spotify Tools

## Current State Analysis
- `tools/spotify.py` implements Spotify control with commands: auth, status, play, pause, next, prev, volume, search, queue, devices
- `custom-tools.json` defines available tools for the agent
- The `play` command currently searches and plays on the default/active device
- The Spotify API's `start_playback()` supports a `device_id` parameter

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Modify `tools/spotify.py`
- Add new function `play_on_device(query, device_id)` that searches for a track and plays it on a specific device
- Update CLI to accept an optional `--device` flag for the play command
- Update the docstring to document the new capability

### Step 2: Update `custom-tools.json`
- Add new tool `spotify-play-device` with parameters:
  - `query`: Song/artist/album to search and play
  - `device_id`: Device ID to play on (optional - if not provided, uses default)

### Step 3: Test the Implementation
- Verify the new tool works with the existing Spotify authentication
- Test device selection with multiple devices available

## Technical Details

### Spotify API
The `spotipy.Spotify.start_playback()` method accepts:
- `uris`: List of track URIs to play
- `device_id`: The device ID to play on (optional)
- `position_ms`: Position to start from (optional)

### Device ID Acquisition
Device IDs can be obtained from:
1. The `spotify-devices` command
2. Active device from `spotify-status` command

## Files to Modify
1. `tools/spotify.py` - Add device-specific play functionality
2. `custom-tools.json` - Add new tool definition"}

name: shell
content:
Spotify Control Tool - Control Spotify playback using your own account

Usage:
    python tools/spotify.py [command] [arguments]

Commands:
    auth                - Start authentication flow (opens browser)
    status              - Get current playback status
    play                - Play/pause playback
    next                - Skip to next track
    prev                - Go to previous track
    volume <0-100>      - Set volume level
    search <query>      - Search for a track/artist/album
    play <query>        - Search and play a track
    play <query> --device <device_id>  - Search and play on specific device
    queue <query>       - Add track to queue
    devices             - List available devices

Authentication:
    First run 'python tools/spotify.py auth' to authenticate.
    This will create a .spotify_credentials.json file with your tokens.

Requirements:
    pip install spotipy requests

Setup:
    1. Go to https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard
    2. Create a new application
    3. Add 'http://localhost:8080/callback' to Redirect URIs
    4. Set SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables
       or edit this file to include them

Spotify Control Tool - Control Spotify playback using your own account

Usage:
    python tools/spotify.py [command] [arguments]

Commands:
    auth                - Start authentication flow (opens browser)
    status              - Get current playback status
    play                - Play/pause playback
    next                - Skip to next track
    prev                - Go to previous track
    volume <0-100>      - Set volume level
    search <query>      - Search for a track/artist/album
    play <query>        - Search and play a track
    play <query> --device <device_id>  - Search and play on specific device
    queue <query>       - Add track to queue
    devices             - List available devices

Authentication:
    First run 'python tools/spotify.py auth' to authenticate.
    This will create a .spotify_credentials.json file with your tokens.

Requirements:
    pip install spotipy requests

Setup:
    1. Go to https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard
    2. Create a new application
    3. Add 'http://localhost:8080/callback' to Redirect URIs
    4. Set SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables
       or edit this file to include them

Spotify Control Tool - Control Spotify playback using your own account

Usage:
    python tools/spotify.py [command] [arguments]

Commands:
    auth                - Start authentication flow (opens browser)
    status              - Get current playback status
    play                - Play/pause playback
    next                - Skip to next track
    prev                - Go to previous track
    volume <0-100>      - Set volume level
    search <query>      - Search for a track/artist/album
    play <query>        - Search and play a track
    play <query> --device <device_id>  - Search and play on specific device
    queue <query>       - Add track to queue
    devices             - List available devices

Authentication:
    First run 'python tools/spotify.py auth' to authenticate.
    This will create a .spotify_credentials.json file with your tokens.

Requirements:
    pip install spotipy requests

Setup:
    1. Go to https://developer.spotify.com/dashboard
    2. Create a new application
    3. Add 'http://localhost:8080/callback' to Redirect URIs
    4. Set SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables
       or edit this file to include them
